papers.title: School phone policies and their association with mental wellbeing, phone use, and social media use (SMART Schools)
papers.abstract: Introduction Smartphone and social media use is prevalent during adolescence, with high levels of use associated with lower levels of mental well-being.

papers.title: From Smartphones to Smart Students: Learning vs. Distraction Using Smartphones in the Classroom
papers.abstract: This study evaluates the impact of classroom smartphone use on student performance through two large-scale randomized controlled trials in China.

papers.title: Beyond distraction: A cross-cultural look at instructors' perspectives on smartphone use in university classrooms
papers.abstract: We explored the usage of smartphones in university classrooms across three different countries from the instructor's perspective.

papers.title: Examining the attention resumption from smartphone to class: a sequential mixed methods study
papers.abstract: The pervasive distraction of smartphones has imposed tremendous challenges on students' attention and engagement inside the classroom.

papers.title: Emotional and Behavioural Factors Predisposing to Internet Addiction: The Smartphone Distraction among Italian High School Students
papers.venue: International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health
papers.abstract: In a digitally oriented society, smartphones provide continual online accessibility to daily life while simultaneously predisposing adolescents to engage in prolonged connections.

papers.title: Teachers' Strategies in Managing the Use of Mobile Phone in the Classroom
papers.abstract: This research describes how teachers manage students' use of mobile phones in the classroom. They need a planned and structured strategies to minimise disruption and improve student learning outcomes.

papers.title: Perception of University Teachers Regarding Student Disruptive Behavior
papers.venue: ACADEMIA International Journal for Social Sciences
papers.abstract: The research investigates university teachers' perceptions of students' disruptive behavior in higher education class contexts.

papers.title: The Impact of Classroom Architecture and Pedagogical Strategies on University Students' Disruptive Phone Usage in Calculus
papers.abstract: Personal phone usage is widespread in university classrooms. Numerous studies have documented how the presence of cell phones in the undergraduate classroom is more harmful than beneficial.

papers.title: Cellphones Put Students at Academic Learning Risk: A Systematic Investigation of Classroom Use, Cognitive Load, and Achievement
papers.venue: International Journal of Recent Advances in Multidisciplinary Topics
papers.abstract: The widespread integration of cellphones into students' daily lives has raised critical concerns regarding their impact on academic learning.

papers.title: Complexities of Managing a Mobile Phone Ban in the Digitalized Schools' Classroom
papers.abstract: This longitudinal qualitative study followed a teacher team at an upper secondary school during the implementation of a mobile phone ban during class.
